zh.lifeSummary: 當Mathew James Parham於1598年出生在英國米德萊可霍爾波恩聖 安德魯時，他的父親Richard de Parham38歲，母親Katherine Brewer18歲。他於1624年與Frances Holmes在美國維吉尼亞州素利結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1655年住在美國維吉尼亞州查爾斯 希迪 庫迪。他去世於1663年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利素利享年65歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州素利素利。

nameMeanings.name: Parham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from any of the places in Suffolk and Sussex, named in Old English with pere ‘pear’ + hām ‘homestead’ or hamm ‘water meadow’. Compare Parm .

variants.name: Parm
meanings: Estonian: from parm ‘horsefly’, an arbitrary surname given by a manorial official.

variants.name: Perrien
meanings: English (Devon): variant of Perriam, a habitational name from a lost or unidentified place called from Middle English piri(e) ‘pear tree’ + ham(me) ‘enclosed plot, (water) meadow’. This form of the surname is very rare in Britain and Ireland. Compare Parham .
Breton: status name from a regional variant of perc'henn ‘owner (of a house)’.

nameMeanings.name: Mathew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Mathew James Parham was born in 1598, in St Andrew, Holborn, Middlesex, England, his father, Richard de Parham, was 38 and his mother, Katherine Brewer, was 18. He married Frances Holmes in 1624, in Surry, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Charles City County, Virginia, United States in 1655. He died in 1663, in Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 65, and was buried in Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Mathew James Parhamが1598年にイギリスミッドレセックスホールボルンスト アンドリューで生まれた時，父Richard de Parhamは38歳，母Katherine Brewerは18歳であった。1624年にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州スリーでFrances Holmesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1655年にはアメリカ合衆国バージニア州チャーレス シトイ コウントイに住んでいた。彼は1663年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州スリースリーで65歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州スリースリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mathew James Parham(이)가 1598년 영국 미들레세스 홀본 스트 앤드루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Parham(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Katherine Brewer(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1624년 미국 버지니아 주 수리에서 Frances Holmes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1655년도에 미국 버지니아 주 찰스 시티 카운티에서 살았다. 그는 1663년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리 수리에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 수리 수리에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mathew James Parham родился в 1598 г. в местности ст андрю, холборн, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Parham было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Katherine Brewer, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Frances Holmes в 1624 г. в местности сурри, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности чарльс сити коунти, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты в 1655 году. Он умер в 1663 г. в местности сурри, сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности сурри, сурри, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mathew James Parham nació en 1598, en St Andrew, Holborn, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Parham, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Katherine Brewer, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Frances Holmes en 1624, en Surry, Virginia, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Charles City County, Virginia, Estados Unidos en 1655. Murió en 1663, en Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Surry, Surry, Virginia, British Colonial America.
